Focus on deliverables and capabilities:

- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):

- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

------------

- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found

-------------

<observation>

  -->

  </facts>

Each fact is ONE piece of information

      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->

  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>

      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:

  <request>Error handling strategy for CheckPlateInfo column count mismatch during demo phase</request>
  <investigated>The CheckPlateInfo() function currently crashes with TypeError when server plate column count doesn't match view column count, which happens when using demo data sources incompatible with the current view configuration</investigated>
  <learned>CheckPlateInfo is an account reconciliation function that should report data format errors rather than crash; the column mismatch is expected during demo/development phases before M0b proto integration; guard-based error reporting automatically recovers when real proto data arrives without requiring manual re-enabling</learned>
  <completed>Proposed guard solution that adds column count validation at CheckPlateInfo() entry, logging the mismatch as an error and returning early instead of proceeding to crash-prone code; compared against temporarily disabling the entire check</completed>

  <notes>The guard approach addresses both the immediate demo data incompatibility issue and a production-level concern where CheckPlateInfo should gracefully handle malformed server data rather than throwing exceptions; avoiding the "remember to turn it back on" technical debt is a key advantage over simple disabling</notes>
</summary>

this.m_plateInfoWithFakeSymbol = this.PreprocessPlateDataForServer( this.m_plateInfo );\\n\\n        for ( let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; ++col )\\n        {\\n            const symbolInfos =    /**\\n     * @ch 預處理盤面資料：統一符號數量並保留完整 CSymbol 結構\\n     * @param serverData 原始服務器資料\\n     * @returns 預處理後的完整盤面資料（包含真實符號和假符號標記）\\n     */\\n    private PreprocessPlateDataForServer( serverData: protocol.ar2esProto.CColumn[] ): protocol.ar2esProto.CColumn[]\\n    {\\n        const processedData: protocol.ar2esProto.CColumn[] = [];\\n\\n        // 檢查輸入有效性\\n        if (!serverData || !Array.isArray(serverData)) {\\n            error(\\\"PreprocessPlateDataForServer: Invalid serverData\\\");\\n            // 返回預設的空盤面\\n            for (let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; col++) {\\n            return; // 尚未初始化\\n        }\\n        \\n        for ( let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; col++ )\\n        {\\n            const actualSymbolCount = this.GetTargetSymbolCount( col /**\\n     * @ch 旋轉\\n     * @param dt 時間\\n     */\\n    Spin( dt )\\n    {\\n        this.m_time = this.ToolsStrip( this.m_time + dt );\\n        if ( this.IsStop )\\n        {\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        this.SpinAll( dt, NORMAL_COLUMNS );\\n\\n        if ( this.StopTime === 0 || this.m_time <= this.StopTime )\\n        {\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n    this.m_reelSoundId = null;\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 旋轉上、中、下所有輪帶，第二個參數為控制單輪\\n     * @param dt 時間\\n     * @param cols 列索引 (0 ~ Game_Define.COL-1)\\n     */\\n    SpinAll( dt, content.thinking:
The user's primary session shows an Edit operation where a guard was added to the CheckPlateInfo() function in SlotReels.ts. Let me analyze what was done:

**What happened:**
- File modified: `G:\Cocos_Project\uk_917_leprechauns_pots_client\assets\Script\SlotReels.ts`
- A guard condition was added to CheckPlateInfo() function
- The guard checks if the number of columns from server data (m_plateInfo.length) matches the view columns (m_allColumns[MiddlePlateIndex].length)
- If they don't match, it logs an error and returns early instead of proceeding to crash-prone code

**Why this matters:**
- This prevents crashes when using demo data sources that have incompatible column counts
- When the real proto integration happens (M0b), the column counts will match and the full validation will run
- This is a "guard" approach rather than disabling the entire check, so it automatically recovers when proper data arrives

**Context from the summary:**
The user was deciding between three approaches:
1. Guard approach (logs error, doesn't crash) - THIS WAS CHOSEN
2. Disabling check entirely (requires remembering to re-enable)
3. Hybrid (guard + dev flag for full disable)

This is a **bugfix** - fixing a crash issue that happens during demo phase with incompatible data sources. The solution makes the validation function resilient to data format mismatches.

Let me create an observation for this.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Added column count guard to prevent CheckPlateInfo crash on demo data mismatch</title>
  <subtitle>CheckPlateInfo now validates column count and returns early instead of crashing when demo server data is incompatible</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>CheckPlateInfo function in SlotReels.ts now checks if server plate column count matches view column count before validation</fact>
    <fact>Guard compares m_plateInfo.length against m_allColumns[MiddlePlateIndex].length and returns early on mismatch</fact>
    <fact>Error message explicitly notes that column count mismatch is expected when connecting to non-game-specific demo servers</fact>
    <fact>Guard allows full validation to automatically resume when real proto data with matching column counts arrives after M0b integration</fact>
    <fact>Solution avoids technical debt of "remember to re-enable" that would exist with a simple disable approach</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The CheckPlateInfo function performs account reconciliation between server plate data and client display symbols. During the demo phase before M0b proto integration, the game may connect to demo data sources designed for other games, causing column count mismatches that would crash the validation with TypeError. The added guard detects this scenario early by comparing the server data column count against the view's column count. When they don't match, it logs an informative error message and returns immediately, preventing the crash. The comment explicitly notes this is expected behavior during demo phase. This guard-based approach is superior to temporarily disabling the entire check because it requires zero manual intervention when real proto data arrives—the column counts will match automatically and full validation resumes. It also addresses a production concern: CheckPlateInfo as a reconciliation function should gracefully handle malformed server data rather than throwing exceptions.</narrative>

    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

return;\\n        }\\n\\n        this.SpinAll( dt, NORMAL_COLUMNS );\\n\\n        if ( this.StopTime === 0 || this.m_time <= this.StopTime )\\n        {\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n    this.m_reelSoundId = null;\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 旋轉上、中、下所有輪帶，第二個參數為控制單輪\\n     * @param dt 時間\\n     * @param cols 列索引 (0 ~ Game_Define.COL-1)\\n     */\\n    SpinAll( dt, 所有 `curTime < NaN` 為 false，與下方 short-circuit `reelStopTime === null ||` 等效，不影響行為\\n        const riseTimeCol = this.m_riseTime * col;\\n        const riseTimeColPlus1 = riseTimeCol + let moveLength = ( ( curTime - riseTimeCol ) / this.m_riseTime ) * this.m_upLength;\\n            this.m_tempVec3.set( x, y + moveLength, 0 );\\n            target.SetPosition( this.m_tempVec3 );\\n The guard validates that server plate column count matches the view's column count before entering the validation loop, logging an informative error and returning early on mismatch.
